title: anthonotha lebrunii
intro: Anthonotha lebrunii, also known as African bloodwood or leopard tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in West African medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Anthonotha lebrunii include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and prevent infections. Herbal preparations of Anthonotha lebrunii are often made by boiling the bark or leaves in water, and can be used to treat a range of health issues, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential side effects.
